title: Fighting Climate Change: International Attitudes toward Climate Policies
authors: Antoine Dechezleprêtre, Adrien Fabre, Tobias Kruse, Bluebery Planterose, Ana Sanchez Chico, Stefanie Stantcheva

tax_mentions.context: Survey asks support for 'carbon tax with cash transfers' and estimates predictors (effectiveness, distributional impacts, household self-interest) plus video treatment effects.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: Section III.A (Figure 10); Section IV (Figure 12); Section V (Figure 16)
tax_mentions.notes: Main policy; also used in causal information experiment (climate policies video).

tax_mentions.context: Respondents evaluate 'carbon tax with progressive transfers' (revenue used for poorest/vulnerable households), and support is analyzed across countries and experiments.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: Section III.A (Figure 10 revenue-use variants); Section V (Figure 16)
tax_mentions.notes: Revenue recycling to poorest/constrained; compared with equal cash transfers.

tax_mentions.context: Includes a 'tax on fossil fuels ($45/t CO2)' outcome, measuring support/opposition and treatment-induced shifts in its acceptability.
tax_mentions.notes: Separate from carbon tax; revenue not specified for fossil-fuel taxation.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: A vehicle ban/regulation, not a carbon or fuel tax.
Public investment financed by debt/spending, not carbon/fuel tax.
Subsidies/incentives rather than a carbon or fuel tax.

inclusion_reason: Directly elicits and models public support/opposition for carbon taxes (with cash/revenue recycling and progressive transfers) and a fossil-fuel carbon tax across 20 countries, including causal information effects on these taxes.
